title: The Instruction Cache Thrashing Loop
scenario: Your anomaly detection model runs on a Cortex-M7 (216 MHz, 16 KB I-cache, 16 KB D-cache, 512 KB SRAM). title: The Bus Arbitration Starvation
scenario: Your industrial IoT sensor runs a vibration anomaly detection model on an STM32H7 (Cortex-M7, 480 MHz, 1 MB SRAM, AXI bus matrix). The system simultaneously: (1) streams accelerometer data via SPI+DMA at 25.6 kHz, (2) runs inference on the buffered data, and (3) transmits results over Ethernet+DMA.

title: The Double-Precision FPU Trap
scenario: You are porting a Python model to C for a Cortex-M7. In Python, you have the line `y = x * 0.5`. In your C code, you write exactly that: `float y = x * 0.5;`. Your profiling shows this one line is incredibly slow. The M7 has a hardware FPU. Why is this math operation stalling the pipeline?
